title: Delete All Occurrences of a Key in a Doubly Linked

content.problemStatement:
You are given a **doubly linked list** consisting of NNN nodes and an integer value XXX.

Your task is to **delete all nodes** from the linked list whose value is equal to XXX, and then **return the modified linked list**.

If after deletion the linked list becomes empty, print −1-1−1.

Function DeclarationFunction NamedeleteAllOccurrencesdeleteAllOccurrencesdeleteAllOccurrences – This function deletes all nodes with a given value from a doubly linked list.

Parameters
headheadhead : Pointer to the head of the doubly linked list.
XXX : An integer value; all nodes with this value must be deleted.
Return Value
Returns the **head of the modified doubly linked list**.
Returns NULLNULLNULL if all nodes are deleted (caller prints −1-1−1).
The input and output formats given below are only if you want to test using custom inputs.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases.
For each test case:
- The first line contains an integer N, the number of nodes in the doubly linked list (0 ≤ N ≤ 10^5).
- The second line contains N space‑separated integers, the values of the nodes in order. If N = 0 this line may be empty.
- The third line contains an integer X, the value that must be deleted from the list.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the elements of the modified doubly linked list in a single line, separated by a single space. If the list becomes empty after deletions, print -1. Each test case output is on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case. The list contains three occurrences of 20. After removing all nodes with value 20 the remaining list is 10 -> 30 -> 40.
Value 50 does not appear, so the list stays unchanged.
The head node (10) is deleted, leaving 20 -> 30.

solution.approach: For each test case we traverse the doubly linked list once. While traversing we keep a pointer to the next node because the current node may be freed. If the current node's data equals X we adjust the previous and next pointers to bypass it and free the node. If the node is the head we move the head pointer forward. After the whole list is processed the head points to the modified list (or NULL if all nodes were removed). The traversal is O(N) and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the number of nodes in that test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space (the list itself is modified in place).
